# Echo Persistence

> EchoMQ enqueues on Valkey — fast and volatile. Echo Persistence is the floor beneath it: an in-heap accept tier (Champ), a transactional page-store built twice (native Elixir on CubDB, Rust on Fjall), and replication to a Tigris bucket behind a conditional-write fence. This is a 14-module / 4-chapter course (plus an overview) that builds that durability ladder from the bottom up; every module teaches one mechanism through an interactive figure, not a table of machine numbers.

## For agents — how this corpus is laid out

This directory is the rendered course tree. Each page exists twice: a readable Markdown source (`*.md`, the source-of-record) and its built sibling (`*.html`). The links below point to the `.md` files. Read the relevant section's `llms.txt` for a drill-down into that section's modules and per-page dives.

Structure: a top-level **overview** (the one idea in three dives), then four chapters, each a top-level folder holding 3–4 **modules**; each module is a sub-folder with a hub `index.md` plus three **dives**.

- **Course landing** — the full map, status legend, and source records — [index.md](index.md)

## Overview — the one idea, three dives

- **Section index** — [overview/llms.txt](overview/llms.txt)
- **Overview hub** — Champ accepts at in-heap speed with an fsync per K records; a page-store commits each batch as one replicated LSN; the model is built twice — [overview/index.md](overview/index.md)

## Chapter I · Foundations — the mechanism and the model

- **Section index** — [foundations/llms.txt](foundations/llms.txt)
- **Module 1 · The durability spectrum** — the one knob (durable records per fsync) and the two axes a queue is placed on — [foundations/durability-spectrum/index.md](foundations/durability-spectrum/index.md)
- **Module 2 · Champ, the accept tier** — in-heap accept with a checkpoint per K; recovery and replay as mechanism — [foundations/champ-accept-tier/index.md](foundations/champ-accept-tier/index.md)
- **Module 3 · Persistence concepts** — Volume, LSN, snapshot, the OCC commit, the conditional-write fence, engine-neutral — [foundations/persistence-concepts/index.md](foundations/persistence-concepts/index.md)

## Chapter II · The local store — CubDB in depth

- **Section index** — [local-store/llms.txt](local-store/llms.txt)
- **Module 4 · CubDB architecture** — the append-only immutable B-tree on one file; the page log on it — [local-store/cubdb/index.md](local-store/cubdb/index.md)
- **Module 5 · MVCC & time travel** — zero-cost snapshots from immutability; reading a Volume at any past LSN — [local-store/mvcc-time-travel/index.md](local-store/mvcc-time-travel/index.md)
- **Module 6 · Replay & recovery** — the log as truth; green boot, replica catch-up, and feed replay as one fold — [local-store/replay-and-recovery/index.md](local-store/replay-and-recovery/index.md)

## Chapter III · The engines — built twice and bridged

- **Section index** — [engines/llms.txt](engines/llms.txt)
- **Module 7 · The native Elixir engine** — `EchoStore.Graft` on CubDB: the VolumeServer, the lazy Reader, the commit-log outbox — [engines/native-elixir/index.md](engines/native-elixir/index.md)
- **Module 8 · The Rust engine** — `echo_graft` on Fjall + OpenDAL: a supervised sidecar the BEAM drives over EchoMQ — [engines/rust/index.md](engines/rust/index.md)
- **Module 9 · Tigris & the fence** — the shared remote: push rollup, create-if-not-exists, log-head recovery — [engines/tigris+fence/index.md](engines/tigris+fence/index.md)
- **Module 10 · The BEAM↔Rust contract** — eg.4: the byte-frozen wire, the backend, the compositional cross-runtime proof — [engines/beam-rust-contract/index.md](engines/beam-rust-contract/index.md)

## Chapter IV · The platform — the whole picture

- **Section index** — [platform/llms.txt](platform/llms.txt)
- **Module 11 · EchoMQ Bus** — ValKey Streams internals and the EchoMQ 3.0 Stream Tier built on them: the verbs, the PEL, retention — [platform/echomq-bus/index.md](platform/echomq-bus/index.md)
- **Module 12 · EchoBus + Echo Persistence** — the composition: bus and durable floor as one system, joined at three seams — [platform/bus-and-persistence/index.md](platform/bus-and-persistence/index.md)
- **Module 13 · Why it beats classical scheduling** — Postgres-backed Oban honestly; what Echo separates where Oban couples — [platform/beats-classical-scheduling/index.md](platform/beats-classical-scheduling/index.md)
- **Module 14 · The door to BCS** — the durable log plus Tables & Properties as a substrate for building systems, worked on Codemoji — [platform/the-door-to-bcs/index.md](platform/the-door-to-bcs/index.md)

## Source records (the course is ratified against these)

Grounded in `github.com/jonny-novikov/fiberfx@echo_mq`: `docs/graft/` (the storage tiers and the two engines — `graft.design.md`, `graft.engine-split.design.md`, `graft.roadmap.md`) and `docs/echo_mq/` (the bus the change-feed rides — `emq.roadmap.md`, `emq.streams.md`). External anchors: CubDB (lucaong/cubdb), Fjall (fjall-rs/fjall), Apache OpenDAL, Tigris object conditionals, and Kleppmann's *Designing Data-Intensive Applications*.
